title: Price Action Knowledge Module
note: Knowledge-first doctrine for AI analysis. This file is not a direct strategy matcher and should be used to improve context, no-trade suppression, and entry-method explanations.

deferred_sources.path: E:/a订单流/book/价格行为/阿布百科全书8800-中文目录.pdf
deferred_sources.reason: Too large for full ingestion in this pass. Use selectively later.

core_principles.summary: Classify environment before promoting a signal bar.
core_principles.ai_use: Always answer breakout, narrow channel, broad channel, or trading range before naming a setup.

core_principles.summary: Breakout, test, pullback, range, and reversal are one behavioral loop.
core_principles.ai_use: Ask whether the breakout was tested successfully, failed, or recycled back into balance.

core_principles.summary: Balance and failed attempts are more common than clean one-way movement.
core_principles.ai_use: Increase no-trade and failed-break weighting when overlap dominates.

core_principles.summary: The best entries often come from knowing who is trapped and where they must exit.
core_principles.ai_use: Name the trapped side, their invalidation point, and their likely relief zone.

core_principles.summary: A trend is often still a trend until the meaningful origin is objectively broken.
core_principles.ai_use: Do not label deep pullbacks as full reversals too early.

core_principles.summary: Signal quality degrades sharply in the middle of a range.
core_principles.ai_use: Suppress entry promotion when edge location is absent.

environment_models.title: Breakout / Spike
environment_models.description: Consecutive trend bars, little overlap, and little time for pullbacks.
no_trade_conditions: Do not fade simply because the move looks extended.
Do not force reversal logic before trap evidence appears.
ai_questions: Is this true displacement or only a loud bar inside noise?
Has there been any meaningful retest failure yet?

environment_models.title: Narrow Channel
environment_models.description: Trend remains strong and pullbacks stay short and shallow.
no_trade_conditions: Do not countertrend from isolated reversal bars.
Do not enter late if stop must sit too far from trend origin.
ai_questions: Are pullbacks still short enough to keep continuation logic valid?
Is the trend origin still intact?

environment_models.title: Broad Channel
environment_models.description: Trend exists, but pullbacks become deeper and countertrend scalps can work.
no_trade_conditions: Do not treat broad channel as a clean breakout environment.
Do not overpromote continuation after pullbacks deepen too far.
ai_questions: Is pullback depth still compatible with continuation?
Are countertrend traders already making money repeatedly?

environment_models.title: Trading Range
environment_models.description: Overlap dominates and both sides often profit only near edges.
no_trade_conditions: Do not open in the middle of the range.
Do not trust first breakout attempts without follow-through.
ai_questions: Is this an edge or just the middle of the auction?
Did outside trade get accepted or fail back inside?

entry_families.title: Gap Bar Retracement Continuation
entry_families.summary: Trade the first meaningful retracement after clean displacement rather than chasing the impulse bar itself.
required_evidence: first meaningful pullback into reaction zone
trigger_logic: Look for stall or rejection in the retracement band.
Prefer early pullbacks, not late recycled retests.
invalidation: Pullback becomes too deep and returns to accepted balance.
The supposed impulse is actually overlapping noise.
no_trade_conditions: Do not label messy chop as a Gap Bar.
Do not buy late recycled retests as if they were first-pullback continuation.
ai_questions: Was the impulse truly clean?
Is this the first pullback or a late recycle?

entry_families.title: Mother Bar Auction Zones
entry_families.summary: Use mother-bar internal zones differently in ranges, early trend pullbacks, and late wedge exhaustion.
required_evidence: clear mother bar plus inside balance
known context of range, early trend, or late third leg
test of outer zone rather than middle
trigger_logic: Fade outer trap zones in ranges.
Use trend-side zones in early trend legs.
Consider opposite-side zone in third-leg exhaustion.
invalidation: Accepted breakout beyond the mother-bar auction.
Context was misread and fade logic was applied in true continuation.
no_trade_conditions: Do not enter from the middle of the mother bar.
Do not apply range-fade logic when outside trade is already accepted.
ai_questions: Is this mother bar inside a range, early trend, or late wedge?
Did price test outer zones or only the middle?

entry_families.title: Major Trend Reversal Through Trapped Breakout Traders
entry_families.summary: A reversal becomes actionable when a breakout side is objectively trapped and confirmation proves their failure.
required_evidence: trap bar that fails the breakout
later body close through the level breakout traders needed to hold
trigger_logic: Enter on confirmation close or on qualified pullback after confirmation.
Anchor stop beyond the trap bar extreme.
invalidation: The move still behaves like a normal pullback, not a true failure.
no_trade_conditions: Do not front-run before confirmation.
Do not ignore larger support/resistance or wedge context.
ai_questions: Who got trapped here?
Has price crossed the point that proves them wrong?

entry_families.title: Caveman Failed Signal Bar Trigger
entry_families.summary: A very strong signal bar gets immediately negated by a stronger opposite trigger bar.
required_evidence: trigger bar clearly closes beyond the signal bar's open or extreme
enough room for scalp or 1R target
trigger_logic: Enter at trigger close, breakout of trigger, or qualified retest.
invalidation: Trigger only opposes the bar but does not truly negate it.
no_trade_conditions: Do not use in overlapping chop.
Do not use if the signal bar itself lacks commitment.
ai_questions: Was the signal bar truly strong?
Did the trigger fully negate it?

entry_families.title: FOFS: Failure Of Failure Is Swing
entry_families.summary: When the market fails a failure setup, a larger swing can emerge because both short-term narratives are invalidated.
required_evidence: second side attempts to exploit it
trigger_logic: Use midpoint or control-line reclaim plus follow-through as confirmation.
invalidation: The market remains balanced without control shift.
no_trade_conditions: Do not label ordinary chop as FOFS.
Do not ignore whether control actually shifted.
ai_questions: What failed first?
What was the failure of that failure?

entry_families.title: H1 / H2 / L1 / L2 Pullback Entries
entry_families.summary: Pullback counting becomes useful only when trend context is still valid and the counting itself is not degraded by noise.
required_evidence: real pullback rather than random overlap
valid first or second attempt after pullback
trigger_logic: Use second attempts when the first was noisy or premature.
invalidation: Counting continues inside a degrading channel that has become a range.
no_trade_conditions: Do not count endlessly inside chop.
Do not use a weak H1/L1 as if it were equivalent to a strong H2/L2.
ai_questions: Is this real trend continuation context?
Is the count valid, or is the market already a range?

entry_families.title: Final Flag And Late-Trend Reversal
entry_families.summary: A continuation-looking pause late in trend can become a reversal trap once continuation fails near objectives.
required_evidence: context showing reduced follow-through or nearby objective completion
trigger_logic: Wait for continuation failure, not just old-trend age.
invalidation: The market is only pausing and continuation is still intact.
no_trade_conditions: Do not treat every late pause as a final flag.
Do not countertrend without proof that continuation failed.
ai_questions: Is this just a normal flag, or a final flag near objective completion?

entry_families.title: Range Edge Reversal And Failed Break Fade
entry_families.summary: In established ranges, failed breakouts at edges are often higher quality than first breakout attempts.
trigger_logic: Enter after rejection or accepted re-entry back inside the range.
invalidation: Breakout follow-through is too strong to treat as a fade.
no_trade_conditions: Do not enter from the middle of the range.
Do not keep fading after the market already converted to accepted breakout.
ai_questions: Is this still a range?
Did breakout traders get trapped, or are they winning?

entry_families.title: Buy Zone / Sell Zone Memory
entry_families.summary: Prior signal bars and later extremes create relief zones where trapped traders exit and responsive traders fade.
required_evidence: later move that trapped the original side
trigger_logic: Use the revisit to ask whether relief flow and responsive flow align.
invalidation: There is no real trapped-inventory story behind the zone.
Zone is hit during already accepted breakout conditions.
no_trade_conditions: Do not invent zones without trapped-inventory logic.
Do not assume every prior extreme will still defend.
ai_questions: Whose cost basis lives in this zone?
Will this revisit produce relief exits, fresh initiative, or both?

entry_families.title: Opening Reversal And Early Session Trap
entry_families.summary: The first opening drive often needs to be separated into continuation, correction, or trap before any entry is promoted.
required_evidence: reversal or failed continuation becomes visible
trigger_logic: Prefer entries after the opening narrative proves itself, not at the first burst.
invalidation: The opening drive remains accepted and never loses hold quality.
no_trade_conditions: Do not chase the first opening burst blindly.
Do not confuse overnight correction with full day control.
ai_questions: Was the opening drive real day control, correction, or trap?

shared_no_trade_rules: Do not promote a setup from the middle of a range.
Do not trust weak signal bars unless context is exceptional.
Do not call every deep pullback a reversal before origin failure is visible.
Do not chase a breakout before follow-through or retest quality appears.
Do not use range-fade logic once outside trade is already accepted.
Do not use reversal logic only because the market feels too far.
